## References


[1] Shamim MS (2020). The Quantum of Solace in Ethics Education.. *PubMed*.

[2] Shelley-Egan C, & De Jong E (2025). From Nano to Quantum: Ethics Through a Lens of Continuity.. *PubMed*. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11948-025-00557-w

[3] Shiratori H, Shinkawa H, ... & Naruse M (2023). Asymmetric quantum decision-making.. *PubMed*. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-023-41715-z

[4] Dalla Chiara ML (2010). Uncertainties.. *PubMed*. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11948-009-9180-z

[5] Barnett SM, & Jeffers J (2024). The quantum theory of light.. *PubMed*. https://doi.org/10.1098/rsta.2023.0349

[6] Shams M, Choudhari J, ... & Rehman S (2023). The Quantum-Medical Nexus: Understanding the Impact of Quantum Technologies on Healthcare.. *PubMed*. https://doi.org/10.7759/cureus.48077

[7] Hollin G, Forsyth I, ... & Potts T (2017). (Dis)entangling Barad: Materialisms and ethics.. *PubMed*. https://doi.org/10.1177/0306312717728344

[8] Nabil M, & Megahed F (2024). Quantum Dot Nanomaterials: Preparation, Characterization, Advanced Bio-Imaging and Therapeutic Applications.. *PubMed*. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10895-023-03472-0

[9] Mohamed AA, Sargent E, ... & Lucke-Wold B (2025). Quantum Computing in the Realm of Neurosurgery.. *PubMed*. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.wneu.2024.09.131

[10] Sharma M, Kumar C, ... & Khatri M (2024). Neurological effects of carbon quantum dots on zebrafish: A review.. *PubMed*. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroscience.2024.10.016
